channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 48 replies — 192.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-05-16 04:33 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224392
>>62224277
that sounds like the right path to be taking to find that extra quality filter. maybe you need more testing time to find that last filter piece. i've learned that more criteria is not always yielding more efficient results though, you can end up filtering out alot of good trades and reducing trade volume too much. i dont think AI is good for idea generation for trading or signals, personally i use AI to run all the math stats and simulations exclusively.

>would take weeks or months to pass evaluations, then even more time to reach payouts.
I passed most evaluations in 1 week to 8 weeks. i feel like if you're trying to pass in less than a week your size is prob too big. the 1 week pass was during a highly volatile period where i got much bigger wins.

It seems like you're on the right track. in trading, usually just doing 1 or 2 things really really well is better than having too many tools. i still disagree with your core stats of 1:1RR and requirements of high win-rate. i know theres traders that make that work but i've always wanted 1 big win to pay for 3+ losers. the big fish arent always biting but when you land 1 you realize chasing small fish all the time is a waste.

62224555
>>62224392
Everything you mentioned: sizing down, different contract sizes, different RR models, higher RR/lower win rate systems, and higher win rate/lower RR systems… I’ve already considered, tested, or looked into.

The main issue is the math. If it takes me around five weeks to pass a typical $50,000 evaluation with a $3,000 profit target, that works out to about $120 per trading day. If I then use the same strategy on a funded account, which makes sense since the rules are usually very similar, that same strategy is still only producing about $120 per day. After the prop firm split and taxes, that is well under $100 per day, which is not really viable long term or enough for financial independence.

The only way to make that work would be running multiple accounts, but that creates the same problem, just spread across many accounts all of which cost money to start and activate. If the strategy hits a deeper drawdown period, I could lose all of them at once.

So the issue is that I either size down so much that I spend thousands on evals, resets, and activation fees just to withdraw a few hundred dollars later, or I need to find a strategy that can grow more aggressively.

That is why I’m focused on a higher win rate + a higher profit factor. A lower win rate strategy might still have good EV on paper, but it also means more losing streaks and more resets along the way. A higher win rate gives me a better chance to build a cushion before those rough periods come.

With that being said, I’m not going to pretend I know everything. After all, I’m the one who came to this board asking for help.

The truth is, I don’t currently have a strategy with the kind of stats you’re describing (3:1RR) The one I have now, with its current win rate and profit factor, is the best I’ve been able to build so far hence why I’ve been sticking with it.

62224557
>>62224267
1% a day means 500% returns after just half a year. If you got a strategy like that then you’d be flying in a private jet within a few years lmao

62224754
>>62224555
>running multiple accounts, but that creates the same problem, just spread across many accounts all of which cost money to start and activate.
most prop firms let you have multiple accounts and if the economics are good enough for 1 you should always max the number of accounts for better scale. other prop firms just give you a bigger account (100k or 250k). yeah averaging $100 per day for 250 trading days per year is only $25k but ideally you get to 5x scale within the first 2 months then thats $125k per year. you can grind that out for years or find the next level of scale like trading for clients, another prop firm, or cut a deal with the prop firm you scaled up with.

idk if you're all in on this or its a side-job and you wage but its always about leverage past credibility & performance record to gain greater scale until you manage an uncomfortable amount of money.

62227720
>>62224754
>if the economics are good for one

Well anon, the economics AINT good enough for one hahaha. that's what I'm trying to solve. It's the question I asked in the very first post.

62228083
>>62224207
I am self taught and very well versed in quant the highest sharpe ive achieved is 1.9

62228084
>>62224557
that means hes larping and did the ting

62228111
>>62228084
It feels good to be doubted because what you've done amazes someone. Thanks for giving me the inspiration to keep at it.

62228122
>>62228083
Okay, here's my conundrum then: Suppose you have a list of 10K+ trades spanning from 2020 until now. Each trade has its data attached: date, entry, PnL, and any other metrics that might be important.

How would you go about finding some method or filter to cut out the low-quality trades from the high-quality trades? Ultimately, my strategy relies on momentum, so days where price chops heavily hurt me badly.

Perhaps I could find a way to either identify a chop day before it happens, or find a new addition or advanced criteria that would allow me to dynamically adjust my profit target and stop-loss based on what the model forecasts for that day.

Those are two ideas I have, but I haven’t been able to implement them simply because I’m not sure how to go about it.

I’ve checked the data and found that even the losing trades often have some degree of favorable movement before they eventually lose. That makes me wonder if I could find something that would tell me to reduce my profit target size for that day based on XYZ criteria.

Again, I’m kind of just spitballing because I genuinely don’t know where else to go with this. Everything I’ve tried to improve win rate by cutting losses has inevitably cut out winners as well. So I’m not sure what the right approach is.

62228424
bump

62228603
>>62228122
Low quality and high quality? That’s any MFT Strat lol

62228693
>>62228603
also if i help i get nothing from this

62228705
>>62228603
the strategy is not anything that would require low latency at least to my knowledge since the avg trade lasts anywhere from half an hour to several hours. again, i got no idea where to go from here. any tips would be appreciated

>>62228693
well if you helped me improve it, you could do whatever you want with it. It's not some proprietary strategy that requires some exotic data, it's pretty simple and generic. maybe that's why it's had a minor edge for so long. I would only be trading on it using prop firms, their sim accounts don't trade on live markets. and even if i did, i'm only trading NQ which is hella liquid. And as I've mentioned before, once I make a certain amount of money I'm done for good. Never gonna trade again. In theory if I could get this thing to a workable state, scaling it up would take a few months and I'd hit my goal within a year or two.

62228733
>>62224557
Depends on capital restrictions, an arbitrage or HFT strategy can easily get 1%/day but probably has limits on how much capital it can grow into.

62228952
>>62228733
lol

62228956
add 5% annual IBKR fees itll auto divide for margin or funding then 10 ticks of slippage

62229415
I still have yet to see someone who can help.

>>62228733
1% a day seems impossible in the long run. No strategy can have only good days with no bad days.

>>62228956
If they are buying and selling contracts to such an extent then fees would def eat into it. But if it’s a single buy or sell, or a position that’s held for hours then I don’t see why not.

62229502
Bump

62230495
>>62229415
I coded up orb it doesnt beat fees the original paper is a meme in the algorithmic trading community

try on TQQQ

62230578
>>62230495
if you want to talk serious share discord username

62230627
Based OP, a genuinely original effort thread with potential. Will continue to lurk for interest and hopes to obtain some knowledge, but this is out of scope

62232509
>>62230495
It had basis on ORB and VWAP but isn't just that.

>>62230578
Before any of that I'd like to hear your ideas on what you'd do / suggest for me to improve the strategy. I've already tried a lot of things, so I'd like to know how you could help me.

>>62230627
Thanks, but I haven't really gotten anything good out of this thread yet hahaha

62232797
bump

>>62230627
Also I thought about this a bit more, edge erosion is a real thing. The ironic thing is that if I released it with the intention of helping others, some actual firm might come along and pick that up and run with it. I recall there was this one strategy I was researching a while back. It performed well from 2016 all the way until 2020. But after 2020 till now it started performing worse and worse and worse. Until 2025 and 2026 where the returns were so ass that the only way you'd profit off of it was to essentially scalp it down to the ticks. Only a group with low latency and a huge bankroll would profit off of that. I saw live how a strategy had it's edge eroded.

I'm not claiming what I have is something that amazing. but that is a worry. At the most I'd share it with close friends and family and help them succeed. After all, what is the point of wealth if not to help others.

62233623
Bump

62234201
Bumpinonce more before I sleep

62236353
bump once more. is there one out here who's got the smarts?

62237934
>>62236353
all im going to say is your approach is too simple i thought orb had alpha decay years ago lol

62238015
>>62237934
Anon, I'd like some constructive feedback. Regardless of whether the approach is simple or not, the fact of the matter is that the data backs it up. Only one of the lines uses ORB.

The full blend, as shown in the first picture, is split across 5 different lines. The correlation between them, while not perfectly non-overlapping, is still low enough that it’s rare for them all to lose on the same day.

In the days since I made this, I was actually able to find a way to further cut down a few outliers, reducing total trades taken by 16% while increasing points by about 9%.

But these are just marginal improvements, and I’m hesitant to keep chasing them due to fears of overfitting.

My hypothesis is that, with how many trades I take, I should be able to quantitatively chop down the worst of them. I’ve already managed to do that with relatively simple filters over the past few days, but I’m running up against a wall, so I want to avoid going too far. THAT's why I made this thread. In hopes of finding someone with knowledge.

62238067
>>62238015
ok, now we're getting somewhere. you are afraid of overfitting and not even using ML lmfao noob

62238083
>>62238067
Rather than implement something I have no understanding of, I'm sticking to what I do know. I'll say it again. if you wanna offer criticism or advice, keep it constructive. just saying "ML" is so vague. Be more specific. ML in what way?

item.title: remember when LINK cucks spent the entirety of 2022 — 18 replies — 34.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
remember when LINK cucks spent the entirety of 2022

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-16 04:11 UTC

OP

blaming Bulgarians and NEXO for their shitty price performance while calling anyone who made fun of their bags Bulgarians

Early replies (first 3)

62236293
>>62236269
kek fuddie there was literal proof

62236306
>>62236293
are Bulgarians the reason you're still a poor baggie in 2026? lol

62236412
>>62236269
LOL

i remember repeatedly being called bulgarian back then

then when that wore off there was the guy who always posted the na pidgeon language or whatever implying i was african or something. never really understood that one

and on and on it goes... the cult always has a mythological bad guy who seemingly has so much power he can suppress their billion dollar asset indefinitely

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62236572
>>62236412
hahahahahaha I remember that too. There was a schizo Linkie calling people Nigerians. The cherry on top of the cake is that Chainlink panders to niggers and even pays them to participate in crypto conferences

62236597
It's always one more integration until moon.
One more partnership until moon.
One more law passed until moon.
The latest carrot on a stick is the last one. For real this time.
This year is the year. All the breadcrumbs are fulfilled. Or something. Any pattern recognition is just "fud."

62236602
>>62236293
No one needs to fud LINK, sweatie.

62236652
>>62236269
Why even make fun of it anymore? This project has gone nowhere for almost 5 years maybe longer, at this point you're giving this dead project more attention than it deserves

62237093
>>62236652
OP and everyone ITT holds LINK

62237126
>>62237093
Baggie cannot envision anyone not holding LINK (also can't rotate an apple in his head or tell you how he would feel if he'd skipped breakfast this morning)

62237134
i remember being called a bulgarian and it was crazy funny because im actually a bulgarian living in bulgaria

62237144
>>62236269
Cripple

62237499
>>62236269
He unironically won. He shorted link from the top while dumping deposited tokens at nexo and made millions.

62237634
>>62236269
Hey hey hey, I am Bulgarian.

62237906
just admit you lost the game of life, stinkies
admit this and i will recommend to the machine elves to wake you from the simulation

62237923
>>62236269
>>62236412
>>62236652
>>62237134
>>62237499
>>62237906
yikes

62237998
I wish I was Bulgarian

62238007
>>62237998
me 2

62238043
>>62237923
P+C

item.title: Are lots of people(im speaking USA for me) just retarded with money and dont/cant admit? — 19 replies — 22.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Are lots of people(im speaking USA for me) just retarded with money and dont/cant admit?

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-05-16 03:52 UTC

OP

Are lots of people(im speaking USA for me) just retarded with money and dont/cant admit? I get kids, hard times, job loss, emergencies, etc… but solo living doesnt seem anywhere as hard as people make it out to be. Dual income with someone who also is frugal seems like a bonus.

Im talking in the vein of saving money. Im excluding the housing market issue and job market for those in specific careers where growth is fucked atm.

Purely to get by, in a safe place, isnt very hard, Especially if solo. I hear co-workers complain but refuse roommates, they get new cars too soon instead of functional cheap used, they got subscriptions out the ass, they fucking travel and stack shit on CC, and buy useless shit. I feel like even bringing this up is the equivalent of just flat out saying.

Like my states COL has gone up 100-150% across the board. I have to move but see it as a saving opportunity. support networks generally dissolve heavily for most in 30s to 40s unless you are insanely fortunate. Marriages, kids, sick relatives, yes these are reasons to stay for some but im talking about those where that isnt the concern, where just staying for known “comfort” isnt worth it long term.

Its also not just my own life im talking about, people really dont seem to want to sacrifice anything when even a little goes a long way. Food is the most basic but fucking insane one to me. How is a single person spending $100 a week on food? Mines like $80 for 2 weeks and I can go even cheaper, in a HCOL(scaled to wages, the cost went up, not wages, only those on the property ladder benefitted)


Am I ignorant?

Early replies (first 3)

62236887
>>62236793
my wife spends $300 a week on food. I tell her I'd be eating ramen in our current situation if she weren't around.

62236933
>>62236793
>$100 week on food
Honestly my man, that’s literally the bare minimum you can spend. I’m assuming you’re ignorant/blind to the poison we’re being fed.

62236974
>>62236933
I cook everything and wait for sales. I got 24 good eggs for $8. Bake own bread, 2 loafs, like $4 no poison. Bundle of bananas weekly, like 5$. 2 weeks of breakfast, $19

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62237102
>>62236974
Life is for living, not just surviving

62237153
>>62237102
But im saving shitloads so I can have better living later. Its why I saved so much prior because I had tons of medical shit for 5 years and a few ER visits. I would have medical debt. Instead im not and making a move.


My friends dad sacraficed a lot so he could help my friend get her first house and all he does is travel right now.

62237162
>>62236793
>How is a single person spending $100 a week on food?
Retards who earn $12/hour will buy a $25 doordash lunch and not realize they just gave away 2.5 hours of their life (after taxes) for a lunch they should have skipped because they are obese.

62237249
>>62236793
>Are lots of people(im speaking USA for me) just retarded with money and dont/cant admit?

Yes. It's quite easy to show this by looking at the percentage of people who continue to live paycheck to paycheck despite making $500k+. If people had their shit together then those living paycheck to paycheck should essentially fall off after higher incomes (accepting differences in cost of living areas, different family sizes etc).


>How is a single person spending $100 a week on food?

Honestly easy as shit if you're not frugally oriented. If you eat unprepared oats, rice, beans, frozen broccoli and a little chicken and eggs 90% of the time, never eat out, and never pay for any beverages besides water, sure, you can be cheap.

If you say "hey I'd like some salmon tonight", "I would like to buy hot coffee", "I would like to eat at an actual, full service, not a chain restaurant where I sit down and order an appetizer and an entree and the waiter brings it to me" easy peasy to go to several hundred a week

62237262
>>62237162
I think a lot of the population really can't do math so they just see "$25" and think "I have more than that in my account" and "it is normal to order doordash".

Like tens of millions of people just make decisions based on vibes. Same deal with getting a 10 year car loan at 20% interest on some shitbox truck, they see other people do it so they go to the salesman and ask how they can do it and they decide their income makes it possible to pay for it so they do.

62237268
>>62236793
>Am I ignorant?
your criticisms are correct but only apply to a narrow age and health demographic.

most people don't fall within that age range or health profile.

But for the people that do, yes. They should be doing a lot more to save money because in a matter of 10-20 years they're going to age out of the ability to earn money with their physical labor and have to find ways to survive with their brains.

the other thing you're ignoring is that people do only what they have to do. At your age people don't have to find ways to earn more and save more, so they don't. When they get older and have to be more efficient, most of them will.

62237294
>>62236793
there's also the fact that about 1/3 of americans are on government assistance of some sort, and those people are actually not allowed to earn or save more money.

there's a gap where you need to increase your earnings enough to cover the lost assistance, and for many that gap is impossible to cross.
they can't make enough to survive, so they stay on welfare making and earning very little.

these people aren't going to tell you they're surviving on government assistance most of the time. You'll just see them not making or saving much money and think they're stupid when it may very well be on purpose to keep their assistance.

62237308
>>62236793
>Im excluding the housing market issue and job market for those in specific careers where growth is fucked atm
Oh sure if you just exclude the two biggest problems then everything looks hunky dory

62237319
>>62236793
insane that amerikeks really consider it normal to share their home with some brown stranger. even on neetbux people don't have that, hell even students these days don't do that anymore and theres endless horror stories from those that did it in the past

62237342
>>62237319
You go into section 8 on neetbux or a very low cost area.

62237356
>>62237342
i'd be more bummed to pay an whole european salary to share my home with ceos and engineers

62237856
>>62237268
retirees without savings or pensions living in social security are forced to live cheap, many are struggling, then they realize “shit i should’ve been smarter when I was younger”

Being old and poor is way worse than being young and poor

62237953
People waste all kinds of money on shit like excessive dining out, expensive college bills, luxury cars, beach houses, unnecessary medical expenses, and failed business investments. Most people also buy waaaay more house than they need. You should only buy the cheapest house that you can be happy with. A town home in a decent area is sufficient for most.
Way too many people concerned with showing wealth while they actually have negative net worth.

62237967
>>62236974
I bought 18 eggs for 2.50 the other dya

62237980
>>62237856
>Being old and poor is way worse than being young and poor
so true.

old people have had longer to get lucky though. It happened a lot in the past. You look at boomers with million dollar houses. That wasn't planning, that was dumb luck. Nobody bought a house expecting to make millions just owning it. That never happened before.

so yeah, being old and poor sucks, but just by pure luck a lot of old people end up doing ok.

62238004
>>62237249
Salmon is cheap, $8.99 a lb is nothing assuming you cook it yourself.
Salmon + pasta is a whole meal for cheaper than $11, I’ve been cooking it for 15 years now. Sometimes tuna filets or snapper instead, but usually salmon

62237929

62237940

62238034

62238040

>>62229879

62229952

XRP TO FUCKIN' $1,000!!!

>after Xi-Trump just happened

item.title: China expanding its industrial dominance, warns US business group — 3 replies — 8.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
China expanding its industrial dominance, warns US business group

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-16 04:21 UTC

OP

>The US Chamber of Commerce has warned that countries have only a “finite” window to respond to Chinese policies that are deepening reliance on its supply chains and harming the global economy.
>The Washington-based lobby group said Beijing was “doubling down” on state intervention in manufacturing, services and frontier technologies.
>It said China was ushering in a “new phase of global impact” marked by rising trade dependence and a rapid global expansion by its companies. It is also using tools such as export controls to entrench its position in global supply chains and counter foreign diversification strategies.
>The warning came in the preface to a report on new Chinese industrial policies produced for the chamber by Rhodium Group, a consultancy. The chamber said the world had underestimated previous Chinese policies, including the Made in China 2025 programme to make the country more self-reliant in critical technologies.
>“The challenge the world now faces is not the result of an intelligence gap...Reports were published. The warnings reached senior levels of government and industry across major economies. Yet in too many cases, the response was insufficient,” the chamber concluded.
>Rhodium said China’s industrial policy was evolving from sectoral intervention to an “industrial policy of everything”. It said Beijing wanted to extend its dominance in industries such as critical minerals and magnets to a broader range of industrial products. Beijing was also putting more attention on services, it added.
https://archive.ph/snjef

Early replies (first 3)

62238059
>The report said sustained government support and weak domestic demand had driven a rapid expansion of its goods trade surplus — doubling to $2tn since 2019 — in what some have dubbed “China Shock 2.0” as the country rapidly moves away from an economy based on low-cost manufacturing.
>It said China was making significant gains in industries such as chemicals, machinery and industrial equipment, following earlier significant expansion of market share in industries such as electric vehicles and clean energy.
>“Global reliance on Chinese supply chains is deepening across a growing number of critical products,” Rhodium said, adding that China was using regulation and economic coercion to reinforce control over key supply chains. “The window for effective policy response is narrowing,” it added.
>Camille Boullenois, lead author of the report, told the FT that China’s evolving industrial policies posed a “real threat” to the economic engine of countries such as Germany and other advanced industrial economies.
>“China’s rise is broadly eroding some of the last areas where they still have a technological and industrial edge, like chemicals, autos, machinery and robotics,” she said. “China is gaining market share incredibly fast in these sectors. If countries don’t react now, the industrial landscape could look very different in just a few years.”
>The report noted that China’s most recent five-year plan had for the first time included a focus on advanced technologies such as biomanufacturing, nuclear fusion energy and brain-computer interfaces. This suggested that its industrial policy was evolving from focusing on strategic sectors to a “broader effort to reshape the entire industrial ecosystem”.
>The trade surplus growth represented success moving up the production value chain and exporting high-tech goods but also its success substituting domestic products for imports.

62238066
>>62238057
ai robots are coming. wake up and invest /biz/.

62238069
>>62238057
China is the new power and Usa is just coasting on past fumes. Only 10% of Americans are doing well and are carrying the economy and countru.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: If rates go double digits in the next 3 to 4 years, parking your money in an interest bearing savings account would actu… — 3 replies — 1.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
If rates go double digits in the next 3 to 4 years, parking your money in an interest bearing savings account would actu…

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-16 02:19 UTC

OP

If rates go double digits in the next 3 to 4 years, parking your money in an interest bearing savings account would actually make a lot of sense, no? That would mean you would get 10%+APY on your networth.

Early replies (first 3)

62237404
>>62237401
>If rates go double digits
they won't. it would implode the government.
the goal is to inflate away the debt, so a melt-up in assets is inevitable

62237750
>>62237401
the stockmarket would fall in the lead-up to that 10%+ interest rate hike. and that'd would result in the stockmarket's average return being like 15%.

62237850
>>62237401

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Australia is fucked — 50 replies — 138.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Australia is fucked

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-05-16 04:21 UTC

OP

>CGT discount - DENIED
>Negative Gearing - DENIED
Where were you when the Australian dream died?

Early replies (first 3)

62224090
have the liberals said they will reverse it? do we just hold and wait?

62224122
>>62223850
Doesn’t this come into effect in 2027? Should I sell all my crypto before 2027?

62224148
>>62223850
its tiring living here
the new rules favour low return long duration assets. high growth assets like btc and leveraged etfs get smoked compared old rules. run a few tests... under 12 months nothing changes.
i wonder if it actually encourages speculation. might as well punt on options or asx bets miners or U.S letfs... you need BIG wins now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224712
I'm of two minds on one hand I have about 600k AUD of cgt obligations which if I were to use the discount while I can would have to lock in maybe 100k of tax this coming year (yes I'll be at 45% bracket because I'm not a brokie)

On the other hand I'm priced out of the property market (not really but I don't want to pay 900k for a shitty Sydney apartment) that any fucking over of property investors is actually a good thing (for me and people my age)

62224719
>>62224712
Doesnt making $600k in profit automatically put you in the highest tax bracket owed regardless of how much you earned from waging?

62226023
Honestly, everyone should be sticking their money into classic cars, right this instant.

Buy a final edition V8 Landcruiser 70 series, a MK V Supra or a Y62 Patrol Warrior and just sit on it.

Watch this space, its exempt from CGT and may sky rocket in value, especially as we push forward into cucked out 4cyl and 3cyl hybrid cuckboxes.

My cars will now become my investments, fuck the Government.

62229029
>>62226023
retro games for me. if you spend less than 500 dollars on a collectible item it's tax-exempt when you sell it. they have more universal appeal than pokemon cards and many well known games are becoming extremely rare to find in complete condition.

62230860
>>62223850
>>62224346
joke of a fucking country
i wanna get out of here where to lads

62233502
>>62226023
yeah you should definitely "invest" in depreciating "assets" because Labor took advantage of their huge political capital
property is STILL the best asset class in Australia by far

62233578
>>62224702
>official inflation is 3%
>inflation is actually 5%
>your asset goes up 5%
>"actually you made a 2% profit goy, pay tax on it"

It's free money for the government in an inflationary environment which constantly understates actual inflation. Basically every asset will have taxable profits while everybody actually gets poorer.

62233595
>>62230860
Fucked if i know cunt. Stealing you pic too.
>>62233502
I'm buying gold n silver.

62233618
>>62223850
Feeling glad that I moved to the US desu

62233642
>>62233595
CGT change still applies + no passive income + risk(ier)
Rent is going to go up and existing properties may get cheaper as capital moves to new builds, which will only be temporary before investors realise no amount of tax incentives will make new builds a good investment
All that being said, I think Labor loses the election after next (which they'll win) and some of these changes will get reversed

62233646
>>62233642
>expecting liberals to do anything useful ever
lol

62233658
>>62233646
If there's one thing they'll do it's change the CGT tax because it's a layup
Also no one is mentioning that Labor blew past their immigration target for the 3rd year in a row, we have at least 6 more years of infinity Indians pumping up rents

62233662
>>62223850
>>62224281
It’s actually worse than that.
No negative gearing for new buyers, but all the existing owners still get their negative gearing grandfathered in lol.

62233868
>>62224712
You'll get the same discount regardless of when you sell. The new system only applies for gains accrued after 1 July 2027. Which also means that for anything that you sell in the future you'll need to know the price on exactly that date, which will be "lots of fun" for illiquid assets like real estate and artwork.

Personally, if LINK seems to be heading upwards by then, I'll pay CGT on the existing amount and move to Singapore for a while.

>>62226023
My last car (Falcon G6E Turbo) beat the stock market. The poor kid who bought it put himself into ridiculous debt for it.

62234274
>>62233642
All i know is i own a house and investing in non pixelated assets for retirement is still a better option than leaving money in the bank.

62234315
>>62223850
I voted for this

62234422
>>62230860
To where? Everywhere is fucked.

t. Bong

62237110
>>62224122
I thought the discount only affected real estate investments? Does it include any investments including crypto?

From what I've heard it isn't happening until 2027. Gives me time to renovate and sell one of my apartments.

62237297
I'm looking to buy an apartment now. Should I max everything and buy a unit near the cbd for 900k or get a tiny studio for like 600k? Surely not all the new builds are bad?

How do I even save money when shares are fucked? Might as well PPOR MAXX right?

>>62237110
>Gives me time to renovate and sell one of my apartments.
You and everyone else mate. Let me know when you are selling so I know when the bottom is in.

62237384
>>62237110
Everything

62237449
>>62230860
Singapore, I moved to singapore. No capital gains tax LOL richer than Aus LOL got PR LOL WHAT WERE THEY THINKING AAH. But you habe to pay cpf! You can take ypur cpf with ya when you leave

62237658
>>62224281
30% min tax on all capital gains.
Which is retarded some bum makes 100 bucks and has to give 30% away lmao. Should just go into income brackets imho
>>62237297
>Might as well PPOR MAXX right?
I keep seeing people say this. How do you make money if you live in your house? You get railed by stamp duty when you sell.

62237662
>>62237658
actual strat is BHP
fully franked dividends are the future

62237863
not selling any of my shares until libs revoke this shit. Luckily im a bogglehead so ill continue as if nothing happened.

62237955
>>62237658
PPoR doesnt pay tax.
You also dont pay stamp duty when you sell.

Is everyone here a bot?

62237958
>>62237955
Strat is to keep upgrading ppor until you sell to retire or fuck off elsewhere

62238012
Why do you retards think boomers using negative gearing to drive up house prices is a good thing?

62238025
>>62237449
>Singapore, I moved to singapore.
A place like this will be very easy to mass surveil with drones and robo-dogs roaming the streets. Best move is to locate to some semi-remote community in Australia near multiple fresh water sources and get ready to plan ahead for bad days. You want space to roam if things go bad. Sure the taxes are humiliation tier but the land mass itself has other strategic uses that might prove more important down the line. You could also do this in the US but Aussie infrastructure is always lagging a few years which is another plus in this context.

Everyone assumes that we're one-and-done with some covid-style power reach event, but there's no way they don't try that again.

62238061
>>62238012
It's not but the current budget lets them continue doing it

>>62238025
Larp

62238068
>>62238061
Larp insofar as I haven't done this yet, that's correct. But am I wrong?

62233709

62233716
>>62223021

62233736
>>62224655
it will be your opportunity as well.

62233806
>>62229570
I'm from Spain and I live well. Got small biz + doing crypto + forex + stocks. Life is good.

62233821

62233827

62233835

62233986

62233993
>>62225388

62233997
>>62224679

62234000

62234021

item.title: Kraken has moved to CCIP. — 57 replies — 11.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Kraken has moved to CCIP.

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-05-15 23:51 UTC

OP

kek fuddies

Early replies (first 3)

62230910
>>62230891
Time to dump btc from 81 and eth from 2300

62230914
WAGMI WAGMI

62230921
>shovel salesman announces that shovels are useful, actually

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231802
i think it might actually be LINK's time

62231853
>>62230891
>>62231282

Lido too now lol
Fresh off the presses

62231868
>>62231843
It was foretold. Obvious in hindsight.

62231901
cuckolds are embarassing lmao

price + chart

62231946
>>62231853
ALL AT ONCE

62231990
>>62231853
oooooooof

62231992
>>62231946
OLD NEWS buddy

62232282
>>62230891

This is actually FUD. Cross Chain Interoperability Protocol is a load of gobbly gook (I skimmed thoroughly) and chainlink still hasn't proven its original use case (solving the problem of getting data into smart contracts in a decentralized manner).

The very concept of Sergey's oracle system is unproven, although sensible. All this bullshit being put on top is not "exciting" or "value-added," it is FUD. It is a stream of bullshit without any actual real world uses.

You retards are getting excited by someone scooping shit on top of technological marvels. Imagine the first airplane, thats a blockchain. Now imagine a woman jumping on top the cockpit and taking a huge dump. Thats what your getting excited about.

62232408
>>62231646
Saar I'm sooo white saar!

62232418
Wait I thought LINK was a SCAM?!? You're telling me the most upstanding, honest, and forthcoming company in crypto, King Kraken, has literally entrusted their reputation in the hands of a fat russian big mac enjoyer? So LINK is not a scam? Somebody comfort me please...

62232437
>>62232418
the scoundrel sergey is very convincing

62232441
>>62230891
Thats cool and all but.... one small problem.... uhh... price??? Oh...... and... chart??

62232461
>>62232408
that's adem newfaggot
lurk moar

62232525
>>62232282
This so much, all the top financial institutions of the world are getting absolutely gobblecocked by sergey

Now X R P. THAT is the one correct thing they are choosing!!

62236073
>>62231115
Hello mentally ill faggot how are you doing today?

62236163
>>62231853
This is old news isn't it? Lido was already with CCIP. They're just now publishing their rationale behind the decision.

62236196
>>62236073
doing great thanks
oh btw: pee plus see, bitch

62236449
>>62231646
>i have already won at life

yeah for a guy that's "winning" you don't seem very happy about that

>>62231646
>hope you fixed your porn/masturbation addiction though

you're addicted to keeping track of a random online personality jacking off
dude that's fucking wild

have you told anyone irl?

62236468
>>62236449
Hope you're doing well, Adem

62236472
hey guys --- how's the new "integration" going today? lets check the numbers shall we?


>CCIP fees last 24h: $637

bahahahaha

>Link price action last 24h: -5.2%

bahahahahahahahaah holy shit


nobody, and i mean NOBODY, gets viciously c u c k h o l d e d quite like the boyscouts.... and then comes back time and time again, absolutely BEGGING for more

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62236479
>>62230910
Oh my fuckk you weren't even lying.

I hate this gay ass market.

62236498
>>62231117
You know he probably owns more Link than almost anyone else here, hes just be a dickhole for fun because he knows whats coming.

62236508
>>62230891
i saw kraken now allows margin gambling without having to have a huge portfolio. pretty cool imo. as far as link, i don't think about it at all

62236594
>>62236163
Well here's a completely new one.

62236605
OMFG WE ARE MOONING!!!!!

62236616
HOLY FUCKING SHIT IT JUST KEEPS GOING!!!!!

62236626
>faggot ass stinkies gloating over nothing
This thread is now the $9 dollar waiting room

62236630
can't wait until the failure of stink is so obvious, even the delulu marines cannot deny it any longer

they will be forced to admit that poverty is their only bedfellow

62237484
>>62236630
the thing is this grift can go on for about 8 more years, the next bull will take link up to 17 and then 4 years until it bottoms to 2.50-5 dollars, then next bull will take link to maybe 8-9 dollars. so its going to be about 12 years until the marines realize they fucked up.


by then people like chainlink god will just point out hurdles that chainlik has had.

chainlink reserves will be dumped and i pray to god that another competitor comes and takes away some of the defi market share chainlink has got.

item.title: Saylor bought another 14,500 BTC today — 18 replies — 8.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Saylor bought another 14,500 BTC today

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-16 02:37 UTC

OP

STRC just spent 1.2 billion on 14,500 BTC today.

That's about 20,000 BTC bought this week.
It'll be reported in their filings on Monday.

Expect 85k+ price after Monday's announcement.
Saylor cannot be stopped.

Early replies (first 3)

62233588
>>62233472
Not one comment on your post. The fucking 5chan bagholders will miss an obvious 5x opportunity again, but surely the did not miss buying the silver top.
On top of that, did you see what Strive is doing?
https://x.com/Strive/status/2054894518990700568
We ll be getting a 150k BTC soon.

62233663
>>62233588
lol yeah it's typical of 4channel these days no signal all noise.

I like Strive, their chief risk officer made coffeezilla look stupid.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pvZnpppwkoM

62233681
What’s gonna happen to the price when quantum computing breaks all the old wallets?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62233713
>>62233588
>5chan
wut

62233734
you niggers talk like Ai chatbots

62233751
>>62233713
>it's real
https://5chan.app/

62233836
>>62233663
Yup. No alpha here anymore.

62234405
>>62233472
The crabbing around 80k on the charts the last few weeks suggested an "accumulation" phase. Somebody was accumulating at this position without moving the needle.
Now we have confirmation it was Saylor again.
An accumulation sideways phase is often followed by a sudden bull run and then an equally sudden correction. Look for a spike in coming days, and take profit before it dips

62234410
>>62234405
saylor always buy high and sell low

62234415
>>62233681
you are sad.

62234460
>>62234445
Imagine trying to handle gold (analog Bitcoin) what the fuck is wrong with you?

62234663
>>62233734
I was thinking the same thing

62234741
>>62233681
Heard about this a few weeks ago when they supposedly used a research grade QC to crack a very old 15 bit encrypted coin wallet. They said simply it will for crypto into a new realm or it will die. You're a zoomer if you think crypto has been the only currency alternative offered before. Simple.

62237641
>>62234741
Banks and investment companies don't even use yubikey.

Bitcoin is on the cutting edge of security with all the biggest nerds backing it, it'll be fine compared to the rest of the shit out there, lol.

62237679
>>62233472
>a gigantic retard double down on being overleveraged on a dying ponzi scam is bullish news
Lol

62237742
I don't hold mstr but I do like the company and saylor. He's positioning it to be the JPMorgan of the next decade if/when bitcoin turns out to be pristine capital. I would only recommend buying mstr for those with a 15 year time frame. I wouldn't be surprised if it just crabs in the 100-200 range for 10 years. He has to keep diluting the common stock until bitcoin can reach gold parity.

62237873
>>62237641
Do you remember how hard it was to get an entire global community on board to simply double the block size? That only touched the network protocol. Imagine how hard it will be to agree on core crypto algorithms where conversion will be required for every account else they are left behind and lose their balance (by policy or by quantum hacker)

Imagine all the new price signals that will be revealed when most accounts are left behind

Invest in ALGO unironically

item.title: The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History — 81 replies — 8.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History

Open thread • Replies: 81 • Updated: 2026-05-15 18:55 UTC

OP

Historic moment as Chainlink services have been selected to be used by the DTCC! This is monumental to the crypto and blockchain industry. They even pinned the tweet! Wow, to think how far we have come as an industry.

Early replies (first 3)

62224616
Is this good or bad?

62224627
when are people going to wake up and realize that chainlink is the only crypto that matters? when will the fucking price finally go up?

62224642
>>62224627
I think you honestly really needed the spoon feeding from autists here back in 2017 to gather the confidence we have in Link. By the time Link got on the crypto map in 2020 or whenever people were cemented in their cliques for whatever coin they believed in. Most of the crypto space are normalfaggots. I’ve gotten a handful of people to buy, but you can never tell them to go all in cuz they are not emotionally invested in link. I got6 people to invest 300, 300, 700, and 500 and 400 and 1700. Almost all of them

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62227473
>>62224616
bad, sell the news event + token not needed
>>62224627
chainlink might matter, LINK does not

>>62224974
many ICO bros made it back in 2020 at $50 withtheir 50-70k stacks, sure they're no warrent buffets but it was enough to live comfortably, too bad most didn't sell

>>62225125
yeah, as long as the tokens aren't needed you can have top of the NASDAQ sucking Chainlinks dick, people just stopped caring, it's not 2017 anymore. Back then it would've sent LINk to triple digits, but those days are long gone, peoeple wanna see actual demand nowadays, but it ain't coming, because Chainlink /= LINK

62227498
>>62224863
Sui has always been 1k

62227508
>>62227498
jesus dude that's like $10k, even if LINK does a 3x (and that's being generous) you'd only have $30k, what are you gonna do with that? lol

62227538
SERGEY BETRAYS US

SELL SELL FOR YOUR LIVES

62227556
>>62224958
This anon is correct.

62227563
>>62227508
Link will be 1k+

62227593
>>62227563
No it will pump to $30 and dump back down to $5.

62229154
>>62224577
it's big news!

62229156
>>62229154

62229158
>>62229156

62229164
>>62229158
Is that fat bitch* still fudding?

*the woman not Sergey

62229165
>>62229164
She's Nadine Chakar from the DTCC and she's partners with Chainlink

62232247
Bump. They trying to slide the other thread so hard lol.

62232345
>>62227563
lmao
lmao
lmao
lmao

62232462
>>62232345
Swift, DTCC, Fidelity plus the other 600 more.

62232545
>>62224577
how tf did we get to $50 on nothing but with literally everything we are barely afloat at $10?

62232606
>>62232545
because when it hit $50 everything else in crypto was pumping for no reason at all, leading to a terrible crash, and the market has basically been dead for 6 years, with only bitcoin seeing consistent gains and solana's shitcoin casino being lamer and scammier than eth's. When accounting for inflation, this entire market has been on life support since 2021.

62232658
I'm so excited for you crazy kids

62232694
>DTCC settles $2.5 quadrillion per year
>Chainlink gets used for 1% of that
>1% of the settled value as collateral
>Full dilution
>1 LINK = $250

62232746
>>62232545
Did you miss Ari-Sama’s thread earlier this year? It’s being shorted

62232794
>>62232545
the fat fuck scammer has released like 400 million coins onto the market since then, diluting your bags. You got scammed.

62232796
>>62226079
I’m 45 next week

62232803
>>62232694
You factor swift and every single other company ever and plus front running and suddenly the meme $81,000 number doesn’t seem very meme.

62232805
>>62227397
Chainlink gatekeeps itself. I’ve told countless normies about a link in 8 years and about 6 people have bought in. And all but 1 have less than 1,000 and the other has 2,000. I also told some kid when playing poker and he got like half his frat to buy in. The people who I have told who didn’t buy wouldn’t buy anyway.

And anyone who does buy isn’t gonna buy anything more than a sui stack at most because you have to be emotionally invested in any investment in order to buy a lot. I have a very affable personality and energy and even tho I’m totally dumb on anything technical…. People believe me. Oh I also got a chick to buy 3,000 link during covid who has a husband.

62234063
>>62232794
Which is less than most.
Bitcoin for instance released half its supply by 2013-2014, which is also when it pumped the most.

In fact, all cryptos in general pumped the hardest when they inflated the hardest.

62234092
Chainlink is literally a cuckold club full of incels waiting for their "real life to begin". They've been bagholding for 9 years while Sergey (their God) dumped on them.

THE cuckolds of crypto

62234112
>>62224577
No greater fools left i see.

62236050
>>62232694
>MOAC
please give me what ever copium you are taking

62236496
duuuuuude don't question the cult
duuuuuuude this investment is for geniuses only
duuuuuuude partnerships


>Despite its internal culture of obfuscation, Enron projected an illusion of transparency, largely through a sophisticated public relations strategy. The company's charismatic leaders, Ken Lay and Jeff Skilling, were often seen on financial networks and at industry conferences, extolling Enron's virtues and its role as a market innovator.

>The company's annual reports and press releases were masterclasses in euphemism and grandiosity. Technical jargon and ambiguous language created an aura of complexity that dissuaded scrutiny. Questions were deflected with a common refrain: Enron was too innovative to be understood by the uninitiated.

62236514
>>62232694
>duuuuuude if we just capture 1%

you need to watch this https://www.youtube.com/shorts/7Z4-WRmZ8j8

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 101 replies — 6.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 101 • Updated: 2026-05-15 13:31 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62211429
>>62211194
hbar going down to 2c before it ever gets a chance at real traction

62211440
>>62195275
>Chainlink is the future

62211870
>>62211440
Hahaha

62211903
>>62195275
Everyone moved to BTC of course, I dumped my ETH for BTC too, I used to hold BTC because of staking but now I can stake BTC natively thanks to TBV and the likes

62211915
>>62195275
I’d rather keep stacking BTC, then put it to work via native staking / yield layers (like Babylon-style setups) instead of chasing dead charts and hoping for a narrative pump.

62211928
>>62209385
Ok as you say so

62211932
>>62211429
Hbar is okay to hold and buy

62212034
>>62195275
More potential upside on alts for the next six months. Specifically memes right now.

62213245
>>62211440
What's your price target anon?

62214696
>>62211932
All in or?

62216973
>>62211915
It's worth considering anon

62216987
>>62195275

Polkadot

62220595
>>62209854
Are you sure?

62224657
>>62216987
Why polkadot?

62224666
cny/usd is > .147 now w/trump headed to china. every penny that can chase risk on is gonna get horned up immediately. The ONLY thing the US has left is the dollar as a w3@p0n. Plus, i can't afford any more makers mark bottles unless my alts start pumping w/haste, so...

62224894
>>62224666
Crazy

62224972
>>62224666
We're cooked

62226444
>>62224666
Risk-on driven by geopolitics, liquidity, and alcoholism. Peak crypto posting.

62228393
>>62216987
>ICP

62228841
chainlink

62229090
>>62216987
he's also pedo

62232214
>>62228393
And why

62232231
>>62195275
>buying the top of the bull trap
Never change, /biz/

62232233
>>62195275
Alts
If I'm already going for a risky meme-asset like crypto I might as well go for broke. Bitcoin already had its big run, now it's just stock market levels of gains with far greater risk.

62232268
>>62232231
Also, this threads riddled with bots holy shit

62232289
>>62195275
Altcoins for profit, Bitcoin for stable investments.

62233740
>>62228841
I'm not holding this

62234251
>>62232289
Bitcoin for your grandchildren

62234320
>>62195275
BTC

62234907
>>62234320
It's solana
